input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

## 1. `StaticResource` → use `DynamicResource`

`StaticResource` is resolved **once at XAML load time** and never updates. Switch to `DynamicResource` — it watches for dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update at runtime -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryText}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when you swap dictionaries -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
```

Then swap themes by replacing merged dictionaries:

```csharp
var mergedDictionaries = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDictionaries.Clear();
mergedDictionaries.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

## 2. App restarting on Android settings change

Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when `uiMode` changes. Tell it to handle the change itself by adding `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        | ConfigChanges.UiMode  // ← add this
        | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
        | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

Then optionally detect the system change and react:

```csharp
// In App.xaml.cs
Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    if (e.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark)
        SetTheme(new DarkTheme());
    else
        SetTheme(new LightTheme());
};
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` = compile-once lookup; `DynamicResource` = live binding. Missing `ConfigChanges.UiMode` = Activity recreation on theme toggle.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in their .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — This is a common mistake. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead, which listens for changes to the resource dictionary.

2. **App restarts when Android dark mode is toggled** — By default, Android recreates the Activity when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. In .NET MAUI, you need to handle the `configChanges` attribute in the Android manifest (or via the Activity attribute) to prevent the activity from being destroyed and recreated.


Now I'll walk through the specific fixes for both problems.
